title: elaeocarpus roslii subsp. bracteolatus
intro: Elaeocarpus roslii subsp. bracteolatus, also known as the Borneo Blue Granite Flowering Tree, is a rare and endangered species of flowering plant found in the wild, particularly in Borneo. It is valued for its medicinal properties, including its ability to treat fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, due to its high concentration of bioactive compounds. The extracts from this plant have been shown to possess antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties, which can provide various health benefits, such as reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and boosting the immune system. In traditional medicine, the plant is often prepared as an infusion, decoction, or tincture, and its extracts are used in herbal preparations to treat various ailments, including fever, cough, and respiratory issues.
